summary: Gruber announces the 2007 Daring Fireball membership drive as he approaches the one-year anniversary of writing the site full-time. He reflects on the positive reader response to his increased output since going professional, noting that memberships are the site's primary revenue source. The post includes an extensive prize drawing featuring over 120 prizes worth $6,300 from prominent Mac developers and design companies. He frames the membership model as a mutual exchange — readers get better content, and their support makes the full-time work possible.
key_points: Daring Fireball's membership system is the primary revenue source that enables Gruber to write full-time
Readers noticed a tangible improvement in output quality and consistency after Gruber went full-time in April 2006
Over 120 prizes worth $6,300 from prominent Mac developers serve as incentive for the membership drive
The prize list reads as a who's-who of mid-2000s indie Mac software: Panic, Bare Bones, Omni Group, Rogue Amoeba, and others
money_quotes: Starting today, we get to see.
The membership system is the primary source of revenue for Daring Fireball. This web site is better because of you.
Many of you have written to say that the difference since I've gone full-time has been both noticeable and welcome: more articles along with a more consistent and steady pace to the Linked List.
Last year's support was tremendous, and it's the reason I'm still here working on DF full-time today.
key_insight: Gruber's membership drive demonstrates the viability of reader-supported independent publishing, with the Mac developer community rallying behind the model by contributing over $6,300 in prizes.
